author: @justinw1765
text:
Pretty cool, but I think Bamboo wrapped with S-2 fliberglass+high strength epoxy+and some graphene is easier and a lot stronger and more durable/tougher.  And if you spray expanding foam inside the bamboo, you get amazing vibration dampening, and increase in stiffness at low weight. 

   Carbon fiber doesn't make a good wrapping material in conjunction with most other materials because it has very different contraction-expansion properties--so when that paper, bamboo, etc starts to expand with heat and/or moisture, the carbon fiber will contract a bit, which = potential breaking of the core material.  Bamboo bike company Calfee found this out the hard way--they ended up switching to fiberglass.

author: @WesternIronworks
text: So a bike is a great platform to generate electricity. the little generators you make with those magnets and the coils you whined could be hooked up to the wheel of a bike to generate a good amount of power with no resistance. I got an Ebike and if i can get the parts i will add one to the rear wheel and see how much it would improve my range but that wont be for a while. I would really like to see what you could do with generating power while riding an Ebike. (mine is my transportation.)

author: @perkins1439
text: I tack welded two 26 in bicycle wheels together used a 3/8 v belt a small pulley on a weedeater motor I think 2 inch v pulley Mounted the weedeater motor over the back wheel used a swiveling idler pulley and a spring to keep the belt tight on the opposite of the pulling side and it's very reliable and will run about 26 mph and has good hill pulling power I added an extra muffler for quietness

author: @jamest.5001
text: I'd like to see a bike  made from recycling denim, blue jeans! Form a composite like fiberglass or carbon fiber, with steel inserts in the required places use PVC pipes as a form, split into 3pieces at a angle with a dowel inside to hold it together, it  the PVC pipe will come apart  when the dowel is removed, use wax and polish the pipe and the dowel to fit easily, and removed easily.  Make up all the pipes first, maybe add a layer of kevlar as the base.  Wrap the wet denim with plastic pull a vacuum when done laying, and wrap with rubber strap, to keep it tight flat and air free,  use a 1kw hub motor, with the battery and controller mounted on one side each of the rear rim, with a small cargo rack on top with a rear fender,  extend the rear a few inches similar to a 28"-29" bike with  24" rims. And use the 4" wide 24"  bike rims being a beach cruiser/cargo bike 18speed with plenty of power, maybe sneak another 1kw motor up front, use fenders with a cargo rack to hide the size of the hub motor!  It's '250 watts' 😜 each, on 9volts!😆 with a 25ah 72v battery,  more like 2500 watts total!!  Use a steel tube with the flat mounting slot in the forks , front and rear,  using two tight fitting slots plates per side, one welded to the tubes, the other screw to it to support the axel.  About 1/4" for the first one. And (up to) 1/2" for the second one, with screws from the inside, thinner, into the thicker, using  threaded holes, m5,  am so mount the rack and fender,  a clamp on metal bracket for brakes also.  If the denim could be done like carbon fiber, it could be cool looking, and cover the seat in denim also,  with a rear pocket in the middle of it! 😜

author: @trevorgoddard2278
text:
It is of course not legal to use on public roads or cycle paths.
Even more so with a 500 Watt motor.
Here in the UK electric bicycles are only legal if the combined motor power is limited to 250 Watts and the cyclist has to provide some power before the motor is allowed to assist, also throttle controls are prohibited (but the level of assistance is unspecified as long as it is less than 100%)
And at any speed faster than 15.5 mph the motor is no longer allowed to assist.
Otherwise it would be considered a motorbike and would need to pass an MOT, be taxed and require the rider to have a relevant license.

author: @c3N3q
text:
Small wheels and a really high seat post makes it scary. 
I believe the copied frame is a trials bike.
I'm building a similar project. Attaching a hoverboard motor to a normal bike, adapting the cog that came with it to fit on the inside of the front chain ring.
Also, I believe you have the stem and therefore the bars the wrong way, which must make the steering very unstable.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
@bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text:
@AndreaDingbatt Go to All-programes/Accessories/Ease of Access/On-Screen Keyboard; you can switch CapsLock ON-OFF with that
If you've got the parts you'll be able to put together a roller yourself, just take it steady, think each stage through.
Good Luck.
P.S. unless you are going to be making TONS' of rolls/sticks you could just use two flat boards instead of a roller machine.
